seoTitle: Salisbury West vs San Remo — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Salisbury West and San Remo. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Salisbury West vs San Remo: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Salisbury West and San Remo.
heading: Salisbury West vs San Remo — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Salisbury West, Victoria and San Remo, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Salisbury West has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to San Remo (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Salisbury West or San Remo safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Salisbury West has a flood risk score of 26/100, while San Remo scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Salisbury West scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. San Remo scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Salisbury West or San Remo?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Salisbury West has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
